topic: SAA Exponential Cell Separation — c6 change-driven verification of c5 surgical-edit landings (LLSVP parenthetical and finding-clause append)
change_summary: finding_length grew 195 → 308 chars (+57.95%) since c5 review (2026-05-05T18:43:38Z); detail_verdict_length grew 1702 → 1896 chars (+11.39%, sub-20% audit threshold). Sourced from drift-audit.json generated 2026-05-09T01:10:03Z. obe_skipped=6 (WIN-063, WIN-066, WIN-043, WIN-006, WIN-033, WIN-028 already re-reviewed since audit). Diff against c5 git revision (commit 0caa5b31, workspace-sync 2026-05-05T22:06:01Z, ~3.5h after c5 review) shows the decider applied c5's two surgical-edit recommendations verbatim: (1) the LLSVP+timing parenthetical was inserted inside the new detail_verdict_text paragraph at the c5-recommended insertion point ('...no derivation of azimuthal asymmetry (MHD attributes the African–American asymmetry to LLSVP-modulated core flow — a structural mechanism the dome's radially symmetric aetheric vortex cannot reproduce, regardless of when the prediction was made)...'); (2) the asymmetry-falsification clause was appended to finding ('...not first-principles-derived; African–American decay asymmetry falsifies a radially symmetric aetheric vortex regardless of prediction timing'). The same commit also tightened the post-hoc-paragraph dataset citation from generic '(IGRF, NASA station archives, ocean-tide gauges, telluric records, etc.)' to specific '(INTERMAGNET observatory network, ESA Swarm L1B, IGRF-14)' — net detail_verdict_text growth ≈ +194 chars dominated by the LLSVP parenthetical. claim, detail_evidence, verdict, code_analysis tags, TLDRs, detail_extra all unchanged.
our_argument_summary: Std Model Explains. Standard MHD via Terra-Nova et al. (2017) provides the spatial mechanism for SAA bifurcation through reversed-flux patches at the core–mantle boundary; westward drift ~0.2–0.3°/year is flow-driven advection in a rotating spherical shell; African-vs-American decay asymmetry is attributable to LLSVP-modulated local core flow. Dome has no MHD equations, no fluid mechanism, no published τ for its 'exponential' label, and a radially symmetric H(r) (firmament-height function depends only on radial distance r — confirmed in raw-text/05-model.txt line 16: H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619) km) that structurally cannot produce azimuthal asymmetry. Bifurcation rate (30.8°→50.6° over 25 years, ~0.79°/yr) is observational on both sides; verdict is backed by the post-hoc trap (CHAOS-7 publicly available, no independent timestamp pre-registration). c6 verifies that the c5 LLSVP-parenthetical and finding-clause surgical edits landed correctly across the c5→c6 patch cycle. Defense rating drops from c5=3 to c6=2.

relevant_files: raw-text/05-model.txt line 14-16 (H(r) function as pure radial)
raw-text/08-live-power.txt (live-fetch list — SAA decay rate, not separation)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Re-verified in c6 clone. 50.57° remains hardcoded in 05-model.txt. Live-fetch pipeline tracks SAA decay RATE in nT/yr at INTERMAGNET stations (linked to WIN-037/042 family), not angular separation between cells. PRED-013's 30-year-horizon 120–180° band (raw-text/03-predictions.txt:45) is a manual long-horizon claim, not auto-monitored. The dome's H(r) function (raw-text/05-model.txt:16, H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619) km) is purely radial — there is no azimuthal coordinate in the dome's geometry, which is what makes our 'radially symmetric aetheric vortex' characterization in finding/detail_evidence/detail_verdict_text faithful to the dome's own published model rather than our caricature.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: 50.57° remains hardcoded (raw-text/05-model.txt:43). Two-point endpoints (30.8° in 2000, 50.6° in 2025) underdetermine the curve family: linear, exponential, quadratic, and logarithmic fits are all consistent with two data points. No τ has been published in the c5→c6 interval.
data_sources_used: INTERMAGNET observatory network — c5→c6 patch tightened the citation specificity in detail_verdict_text
ESA Swarm L1B — c5→c6 patch added to dataset enumeration
IGRF-14 — c5→c6 patch replaced generic 'IGRF' label with version-specific reference
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: Unchanged from c5: unpublished τ remains the central rhetorical escape. Without a functional form the dome can post-hoc fit any 2055 outcome inside the 120–180° band as 'confirmation' and any outside as 'aetheric damping' or 'overshoot.' The c5→c6 patch did not address this — and arguably cannot, because closing the escape clause requires the DOME to publish τ, not us.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None new. The c3-era live_fetch / hardcoded inconsistency remains resolved (c4). The c5→c6 patch did not introduce any code-claim regressions.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Re-verified in c6 clone at run start. raw-text/05-model.txt:43 carries 50.57° as a static architectural constant; raw-text/08-live-power.txt 20-domain live-fetch list tracks SAA decay rate (WIN-037/042 family), not angular separation. WIN-005 (paired SAA citation, 'African SAA Cell Decays Faster CONFIRMED' raw-text/02-wins.txt:65) shares the hardcoded tag — cross-WIN consistent. No live-fetch path exists for inter-cell angular separation in the dome's monitor script.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Terra-Nova et al. (2017) PEPI 266:39-53, DOI 10.1016/j.pepi.2017.03.002, 'Relating the South Atlantic Anomaly and geomagnetic flux patches,' derives SAA bifurcation from reversed-flux patches and core flow — published before the dome model's prediction commits. The dome relabels the cause as 'aetheric vortex decay' without altering any predicted value. The c5→c6 patch reinforces this characterization by making the LLSVP attribution explicit in the verdict synthesis (data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_verdict_text), strengthening the case that the dome is renaming a mechanism MHD has, not introducing a novel mechanism.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: CHAOS-7 (Finlay et al. 2020) is a public dataset documenting SAA bifurcation prior to the dome's monitor commit. The c5→c6 patch tightened the dataset-list specificity (INTERMAGNET, Swarm L1B, IGRF-14) but did not change the post-hoc verdict — all three datasets are publicly indexed and predate the dome's prediction commit. Author's own predictions-page definition requires timestamping before confirming-data pull; the monitor script hardcodes the target on the same commit as the data-fetch line, which makes the temporal ordering unverifiable from repo history. Public-data carve-out of Part 3.1b does not apply.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Re-verified: PROS-001 (raw-text/02-wins.txt:14-15) publishes 'exponential separation curve' as a label without a τ. WIN-004 (raw-text/02-wins.txt:62-63) confirms with no derivation. The c5 patch added 'the exponential rate parameter τ is unpublished' to detail_verdict_text — that phrase remains in c6 (data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_verdict_text). No derivation path from disc radius / firmament height H(r) / aether coupling κ to the 50.6° value exists in any raw-text file.

holes_found.description: c4/c5 hole #2 has now survived THREE consecutive patch cycles (c4→c5, c5→c6 unaddressed in both). The detail_evidence text retains the unsourced supporting claim 'is consistent with westward-drift advection in broader MHD models' with no specific paper cited (data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_evidence). Terra-Nova et al. (2017) is the only DOI in detail_evidence and it is cited only for the spatial mechanism, not for a published bifurcation-rate prediction. Pattern observation: the c5→c6 patch successfully landed two of three c5 surgical recommendations (hole #1 LLSVP parenthetical, hole #3 finding clause) but skipped hole #2 (detail_evidence sourcing). This is the second cycle where verdict-text framing was patched while evidence-text sourcing was deferred. The c5 recommendation offered two options — (a) substitute Aubert (2015) DOI 10.1093/gji/ggv394, (b) soften phrasing to drop the implicit promise of a rate-prediction citation. Option (b) is the lower-risk patch and requires only a sentence-level word change. A defender can still ask 'name the MHD paper that publishes the 0.8°/year separation rate prediction' and our text has no citation handle. Severity remains minor (not blocking) because the verdict 'Std Model Explains' rests on the structural mechanism gap, not on a rate-prediction claim — but the citation hygiene gap accumulates rhetorical cost over cycles.
holes_found.recommendation: Adopt c5 hole #2 option (b) as the minimum-friction close: replace 'is consistent with westward-drift advection in broader MHD models' with 'is consistent with the westward-drift advection regime modeled by core-flow MHD' (drops the implicit promise of a specific rate-prediction paper). If the analyst can verify Aubert (2015) GJI 203:1738-1751 cleanly, option (a) is strictly stronger — but option (b) is a single-line word change that closes the hole this cycle without further verification work.

holes_found.description: c4 stronger-argument #3 (PRED-013 feasibility math in detail_extra) remains un-integrated; detail_extra is still null after three patch cycles (c3→c4→c5→c6). The math is: linear extrapolation = 74.4° in 2055; PRED-013 lower bound 120° requires 2.92× current rate; upper bound 180° requires 5.45× current rate. This shows the dome's most testable long-horizon commitment requires a 3-5× rate acceleration that has not yet been observed. Survival across three cycles suggests the recommendation is not landing as a high-priority decider work item — possibly because detail_extra is the lowest-visibility field and the math is preserved in c4/c5/c6 review records where any analyst regenerating prose can find it. Severity minor.
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) integrate the PRED-013 math into detail_extra as a 3-line block ('Linear extrapolation: 74° in 2055. PRED-013 lower bound: 2.92× current rate required. Upper bound: 5.45× current rate required.'), or (b) explicitly mark detail_extra as intentionally null and remove the standing recommendation from review tracking. (a) is preferred because the math is genuinely strengthening; (b) is acceptable if the decider judges detail_extra integration is a structural decision rather than a per-WIN choice.

stronger_arguments.description: The c5→c6 patch landed both highest-leverage surgical edits, so the high-leverage stronger-arguments backlog is now empty. Outstanding stronger-arguments are infrastructure (Aubert (2015) verification, PRED-013 math integration) rather than rhetorical kill-shots. The next high-leverage opportunity, if one becomes available, would be a published peer-reviewed MHD rate-style forecast that names the SAA bifurcation rate prospectively — which would convert hole #2 from a citation-hygiene patch to a substantive 'MHD predicted, dome adopted' move. None has surfaced in the c5→c6 interval; flagging for analyst monitoring rather than acting on it.
sources: Aubert (2015) GJI 203:1738-1751 — proposed substitute for hole #2 detail_evidence sourcing; not yet retrieved/verified
Garnero & McNamara (2008) Science 320:626-628 — LLSVP review, supports c5/c6 verdict-text LLSVP attribution

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-WIN reinforcement opportunity: WIN-040 (SAA western boundary) was patched 2026-04-23/24 to articulate the H(r) zero-azimuthal-derivative point explicitly. WIN-004 now also leans on the radially-symmetric-vortex-cannot-produce-asymmetry argument across all three reader paths (finding, detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text). A sentence-level cross-link in WIN-004's detail_verdict_text or detail_extra ('see WIN-040 for the H(r) constraint articulation') would convert two independent arguments into a single mutually-reinforcing structural claim. Out of scope for c6 (would be an analyst expansion, not a curmudgeon edit), but flagged for tracker.
sources: WIN-040 (SAA western boundary) — patched 2026-04-23/24 to articulate H(r) constraint

kernel_of_truth.description: The c5→c6 patch is a measurable improvement to the WIN's rhetorical architecture and represents the second consecutive successful surgical-edit landing. Both c5 surgical recommendations were applied verbatim: the LLSVP+timing parenthetical inside the new detail_verdict_text paragraph (data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_verdict_text — exact match to c5 hole #1 recommendation text), and the African–American asymmetry-falsification clause appended to finding (data/wins.json:WIN-004.finding — exact match to c5 hole #3 recommendation text). The decider also tightened the post-hoc-paragraph dataset citation from generic dataset list to specific INTERMAGNET/Swarm L1B/IGRF-14 enumeration in the same commit (0caa5b31). The post-c5 advocate prediction ('After [hole #1] insertion, the defense rating drops to 1–2') is largely realized: defense_survives drops from c5=3 to c6=2. The structural-falsification framing is now visible across all three reader paths — summary table (finding), evidence detail (detail_evidence), and verdict synthesis (detail_verdict_text) — which closes the c5-flagged 'TLDR-readers miss the kill-shot' rhetorical hole.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The verdict 'Std Model Explains' continues to hold (data/wins.json:WIN-004.verdict). Even with the c6 improvements, defense rating is 2, not 1: the unsourced 'broader MHD models' phrase in detail_evidence (data/wins.json:WIN-004.detail_evidence) gives a defender a citation-hygiene attack surface that the c5 hole #2 option (b) softening would close in one word change. The two surviving holes (hole #2 stagnation, detail_extra null) are textual rather than analytical and do not warrant a verdict change — they warrant the surgical edits enumerated above. Recommended action minor_edit, not major_rewrite.

advocate_mode.best_defense: After the c5→c6 patch landings, a fair-minded dome defender's strongest remaining attacks are narrow: (1) 'Your verdict synthesis claims the standard model has azimuthal-asymmetry derivation via LLSVP-modulated core flow, but your detail_evidence cites only Terra-Nova et al. (2017) for the spatial mechanism. The LLSVP attribution is asserted but not directly cited in the verdict-side text — readers who follow your DOI find a paper about flux patches, not a paper about LLSVP modulation of azimuthal SAA asymmetry. So your verdict claims a mechanism your verdict-side citation does not directly establish.' This pivots on the same citation-hygiene axis as c5 hole #2. (2) 'Your text says the dome is radially symmetric — but the dome model includes asymmetric features elsewhere (e.g., disc-edge geometry, polaris alignment). Maybe the aetheric vortex is asymmetric in ways you have not investigated.' This is rebuttable: the dome's H(r) function (raw-text/05-model.txt:16) IS radially symmetric by construction, and the aetheric vortex per the dome's own framing is driven by H(r). But the rebuttal requires the reader to follow the H(r) thread, which we don't currently cite in detail_verdict_text. (3) 'You acknowledge the bifurcation rate is observational on both sides. So the rate is not a discriminator. Your asymmetry argument is also observational — neither side staked a quantitative azimuthal-asymmetry magnitude before observation. Symmetric retrofit on both sides at the asymmetry level too.' Rebuttable: asymmetry presence (any non-zero value) is structurally fatal to a radially-symmetric aetheric vortex regardless of magnitude — but the rebuttal requires the reader to grant our characterization of the dome as radially symmetric.
advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: Adopt hole #2 option (b) for detail_evidence ('westward-drift advection regime modeled by core-flow MHD' — single-word-class change). To further close attack #1 above, consider adding 'see Garnero & McNamara (2008) for LLSVP review' as a parenthetical citation immediately after the LLSVP parenthetical in detail_verdict_text — but this is an extension, not a defect, and is lower priority than hole #2. After the hole #2 close, defense_survives should drop to 1 — at which point this WIN would no longer benefit from further surgical iteration.

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: None. WIN-004 / WIN-005 still share monitoring='hardcoded', relabels_standard=true, post_hoc=true, derives_from_dome=false (cluster consistent). WIN-040 (SAA western boundary <45°W) was patched 2026-04-23/24 to articulate the H(r) zero-azimuthal-derivative point explicitly; WIN-004's c5→c6 patch reinforces the same H(r) radial-symmetry constraint via the LLSVP parenthetical. The two WINs are now mutually reinforcing on the radially-symmetric-aetheric-vortex argument — flagged for analyst cross-link expansion (out of scope for c6). WIN-035 (African cell intensity below 21,795 nT, raw-text/02-wins.txt:123) is a separate WIN about decay rate, not separation, and shares the post-hoc/hardcoded tag pattern.

quantitative_verification.computations: Ran arithmetic in node REPL inside the c6 clone. Length deltas (308-195)/195 = 0.5795 and (1896-1702)/1702 = 0.1140 confirm the drift-audit.json magnitudes. All c5-era arithmetic (rate 0.792 °/yr, 74.36° extrapolation, 2.92× and 5.45× rate-multiplier requirements, 0.03° post-hoc fit gap) re-verified — no new numeric claims were added in the c5→c6 interval. The H(r) function form re-checked against raw-text/05-model.txt line 16 — confirmed pure radial.

deeper_analysis:
This is a change-driven verification pass triggered by finding_length growth (195 → 308 chars, +57.95%) since c5 review on 2026-05-05T18:43:38Z. Diff-against-git (commit 0caa5b31, workspace-sync 2026-05-05T22:06:01Z, ~3.5h after c5) shows the growth is fully explained by appending the c5 hole #3 recommendation clause to finding. The same commit also added the c5 hole #1 LLSVP parenthetical to detail_verdict_text (sub-threshold +11.4% drift) and tightened the post-hoc-paragraph dataset citation. Both highest-leverage c5 surgical recommendations landed verbatim — the second consecutive successful surgical-edit cycle for this WIN (c4→c5 also delivered surgical edits cleanly). The post-c5 advocate prediction (defense_survives drops 3→1-2) is largely realized: c6 advocate rates defense at 2. Remaining holes are minor and concentrated on a single pattern: the decider patches verdict-text framing readily but defers detail_evidence sourcing (hole #2) and detail_extra integration (PRED-013 math) across multiple cycles. Hole #2 is now in its third stagnation cycle. Recommended action minor_edit (not major_rewrite) because all surviving issues are textual rather than analytical and each maps to a sentence-or-phrase-level surgical edit. Verdict 'Std Model Explains' continues to hold; advocate defense_survives drops from c5=3 to c6=2.

The-other-side check: would a charitable reader of the dome model say we are being unfair? The closest charitable read is 'the dome's exponential label was qualitatively predictive — the cells did separate on a non-linear-looking trajectory.' But two data points cannot distinguish exponential from linear+noise from quadratic from logarithmic, and the dome has not published τ — so 'the curve looks exponential' is observationally consistent with several functional forms, not a unique prediction. Our text already concedes the rate is observational on both sides; we are not strawmanning. The remaining charitable angle would be 'the dome got the asymmetric structural prediction right qualitatively' — but the dome's H(r) is radially symmetric by construction, so any predicted asymmetry would be a stipulation imposed on the prediction, not a derivation from the model. We are being fair.
